suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a lightweight procedure path to ra-engine-worker for housekeeping/refactoring features that skips the TDD cycle (Steps 2-4: spec study, baseline fixture, failing tests). These non-detector features have no new tests to write and the red-green cycle adds friction without value.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both misc-gitignore-cleanup (worker 9e85029a) and misc-post-build-cleanup (worker 28bc5aed) workers reported the same skill mismatch. Their skillFeedback both note the TDD-oriented procedure doesn't map well to config changes, refactoring, and gitignore cleanup.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the HTF timeframe key format convention: config YAML uses '1H'/'4H'/'1D' format while some detector internals and baseline fixtures use 'H1'/'H4'/'D1'. Workers implementing HTF-aware detectors should use the config format (1H/4H/1D) as canonical and provide mapping if needed.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature misc-post-build-cleanup (commit a3ec6b0) added a _TF_TO_CONFIG_KEY mapping dict in htf_liquidity.py to bridge the two formats. This format mismatch was not documented and could confuse future workers.

rejectedObservations.observation: AGENTS.md states config-driven convention but AsiaRangeDetector originally hardcoded thresholds — convention enforcement gap.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented: The convention is already in AGENTS.md ('If you hardcode a threshold, the system has failed'). This was an implementation oversight in the original leaf-detectors milestone, not a documentation gap. The misc-gitignore-cleanup feature corrected it.
